title: Fix ACS connection string missing accesskey crashes dev API
description: SharedStack.cs constructs ACS connection string from hostname only (endpoint=https://{host}/) but EmailClient requires accesskey. Use ListCommunicationServiceKeys to get PrimaryConnectionString. This crashes the entire dev API container on startup.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #166 — infra now uses ListCommunicationServiceKeys for full connection string, API hardens EmailClient registration

title: Fix broken Leaflet marker icons on map
description: Leaflet default marker icons show as ? placeholders due to Vite bundling. Need to explicitly configure icon paths.

title: Cap PlanIt areas page size at 100 and add pagination
description: CachedPlanItAuthorityProvider.cs fetches all ~400 UK authorities with pg_sz=500 in a single request. Reduce to pg_sz=100 and add pagination loop to accumulate all pages. Must never call PlanIt with pg_sz > 100.
close_reason: Fixed in commit fc422e0 on PR #168

title: Cap PlanIt applications page size at 100
description: Change DefaultPageSize from 5000 to 100 in PlanItClient.cs. Update PlanItClientTests pagination test (5000-record page → 100-record page, totals accordingly). Update mock response pg_sz fields. Root cause: PlanIt returns 400 when response > 1MB, and pg_sz=5000 with 30-day lookback exceeds that for most authorities.
close_reason: Fixed in commit fc422e0 on PR #168

title: Fix map page: fetch applications via user authorities instead of watch zones
description: The map page shows no pins because it fetches applications by first calling watch zones, then extracting authority IDs. If watch zones returns empty, no application fetches happen. The applications page works because it uses getMyAuthorities() directly. Fix: update the map port and useMapData hook to use the same user-authorities approach as the applications page.
close_reason: Replaced watch-zone-based data fetching with user-authorities approach matching the applications page

title: Fix PlanIt 400: wrong date format and missing default date
description: The polling job fails every 15 minutes because: (1) PlanIt API rejects yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss format for different_start — only accepts yyyy-MM-dd. (2) When no poll state exists (first run), different_start is omitted entirely, but PlanIt now requires a date restriction.
close_reason: Fixed PlanIt date format (yyyy-MM-dd instead of yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss) and added 30-day default lookback when no poll state exists

title: Extract polling into Container Apps Job
description: Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-04-extract-polling-job-design.md — New town-crier.worker console app, CosmosPollStateStore, simplified handler, Pulumi Job resource, CI/CD, revert MinReplicas to 0
close_reason: Merged via PR #158 — polling extracted to Container Apps Job

title: Add partition key range fan-out for cross-partition queries in CosmosRestClient
description: Cosmos DB REST API gateway returns HTTP 400 with partitionedQueryExecutionInfoVersion when it cannot serve cross-partition queries (DISTINCT, GROUP BY). CosmosRestClient.QueryAsync needs to detect this and fan out to individual partition key ranges. This is blocking production polling - every 15-minute poll cycle crashes because SELECT DISTINCT VALUE c.authorityId FROM c triggers the gateway 400 error.

title: Fix: set MinReplicas=1 to keep polling service alive
description: Container App has MinReplicas=0, so it scales to zero when idle. Since PlanItPollingService is a BackgroundService inside the API container, it dies when the container scales down. Need MinReplicas=1 to keep the 15-min polling loop running.

title: Fix: wire WatchZoneActiveAuthorityProvider instead of InMemory
description: InMemoryActiveAuthorityProvider is registered for IActiveAuthorityProvider but nothing ever calls .Add() on it, so every poll cycle gets an empty authority list and does zero work. WatchZoneActiveAuthorityProvider already exists and queries Cosmos for active authorities from user watch zones — just need to swap the DI registration.

## Infrastructure Change 1: Add Cosmos__DatabaseName env var to container app

### Analysis

Verified the current infra configuration against the API's requirements:

1. **Cosmos__AccountEndpoint** -- Already set at EnvironmentStack.cs:267. Uses `cosmosAccountEndpoint` from the shared stack output. VERIFIED CORRECT.

2. **Cosmos__DatabaseName** -- MISSING. The API's `CosmosServiceExtensions.AddCosmosRestClient()` requires `Cosmos:DatabaseName` and throws `InvalidOperationException` if absent. The infra creates per-environment databases named `town-crier-{env}` (line 71) but was not passing this name as an env var to the container app.

3. **Connection string references** -- Searched all infra files for `ConnectionString`, `connectionString`, and `connection.string` (case-insensitive). No matches found. The infra uses managed identity (via `cosmosDataIdentityId` and `AZURE_CLIENT_ID`) exclusively. CLEAN.

### Change Made

Added `Cosmos__DatabaseName` env var to the container app in `EnvironmentStack.cs`, using `cosmosDatabase.Name` which resolves to `town-crier-dev` or `town-crier-prod` depending on the stack.

title: Migrate all repositories to REST client
description: Rewrite all 9 Cosmos*Repository implementations to use ICosmosRestClient instead of the SDK's Container type. This is a mechanical translation — each repo's constructor takes ICosmosRestClient, and operations map: ReadItemAsync→ReadDocumentAsync, UpsertItemAsync→UpsertDocumentAsync, DeleteItemAsync→DeleteDocumentAsync, GetItemQueryIterator+CollectAsync→QueryAsync, ScalarAsync→ScalarQueryAsync. All methods pass the appropriate JsonTypeInfo from CosmosJsonSerializerContext.Default. Special cases: WatchZoneRepository.DeleteAsync does read-then-delete to preserve WatchZoneNotFoundException semantics. Spatial queries (ST_DISTANCE) are just SQL strings — work identically over REST. After migration, verify full build succeeds, all tests pass, no Microsoft.Azure.Cosmos or Newtonsoft references remain in src/. Repos: CosmosUserProfileRepository, CosmosDecisionAlertRepository, CosmosWatchZoneRepository, CosmosPlanningApplicationRepository, CosmosDeviceRegistrationRepository, CosmosNotificationRepository, CosmosGroupRepository, CosmosGroupInvitationRepository, CosmosSavedApplicationRepository. See plan tasks 6-10.

title: Implement CosmosRestClient with auth and Polly resilience
description: Build the core REST client that replaces Microsoft.Azure.Cosmos. This covers: ICosmosRestClient interface with ReadDocument/Upsert/Delete/Query/ScalarQuery methods, CosmosRestOptions config POCO, CosmosAuthProvider for Entra ID token acquisition via DefaultAzureCredential with caching, CosmosRestClient implementation handling URL construction, headers (x-ms-version, x-ms-date, x-ms-documentdb-partitionkey, auth), query body serialization (CosmosQueryBody/CosmosQueryParameter types added to CosmosJsonSerializerContext), response envelope parsing via JsonDocument for AOT safety, continuation token pagination, 404-as-null for reads, silent 404 for deletes, and cross-partition query header. Also add Microsoft.Extensions.Http.Resilience package with Polly retry pipeline (429/408/503/449, exponential backoff with jitter, x-ms-retry-after-ms header honoring). Full TDD with StubHttpHandler, CosmosRestClientTests (URL paths, headers, pagination, error handling), and CosmosAuthProviderTests (token caching, refresh). See plan tasks 2+3.
acceptance_criteria: ICosmosRestClient interface defined with all 5 methods. CosmosAuthProvider acquires and caches Entra ID tokens. CosmosRestClient builds correct URLs, sets all required headers, handles pagination via continuation tokens, returns null on 404 reads, silently succeeds on 404 deletes, throws on non-retryable errors. Query body serialized as application/query+json with CosmosQueryBody. Cross-partition queries set enablecrosspartition header. All unit tests pass with StubHttpHandler.

title: Fix detect-changes composite action failing on new PRs
description: Since PR #78, the detect-changes composite action fails instantly on all new PRs with 0 steps executed. PRs #80, #81, #82 all failed. Action was created in PR #69 and worked for #69-78. Needs investigation.
close_reason: Root cause was GitHub account billing issue, not a code bug. No code changes needed.

comments.author: AmeDe
comments.text:
## Investigation: Root Cause Found

### Finding
The detect-changes composite action is NOT broken. All four recent PR failures (runs 23688499651, 23688472203, 23688288821, 23688234923) share the identical error annotation:

> The job was not started because recent account payments have failed or your spending limit needs to be increased. Please check the Billing and plans section in your settings.

This is a GitHub account billing issue, not a code/workflow issue.

### Evidence

1. actionlint passes on all three workflow files (pr-gate.yml, cd-dev.yml, cd-prod.yml) with zero errors
2. YAML validation passes on detect-changes/action.yml
3. No workflow files changed after PR #78 (the last successful run) -- git diff 251ecbb..HEAD -- .github/ produces empty output
4. All four failed runs show steps: [] (0 steps executed) and 3-second duration -- the runner never starts
5. The check-run annotations API for all four failed jobs return the exact same billing error message
6. PR #78 run (23688057054) succeeded in 2m0s -- the cutoff happened between PR #78 and the next PRs

### Conclusion
No code changes are needed. The account owner needs to:
1. Check GitHub Settings > Billing and plans
2. Update payment method or increase spending limit
3. Re-run the failed PR checks

### Scope
This bead requires NO changes to .github/workflows/ or .github/actions/. The fix is an account-level billing action that must be performed by the repository owner in GitHub Settings.

title: Fix CorsMiddlewareTests failing on main
description: CorsMiddlewareTests.Should_ReturnCorsHeaders_When_RequestFromAllowedOrigin and Should_HandlePreflightRequest_When_OptionsFromAllowedOrigin both fail on CI. Tests expect Access-Control-Allow-Origin header but it is not present. Pre-existing bug on main, latent because recent PRs did not trigger API tests. Any PR touching workflow files or API code will fail the gate. File: api/tests/town-crier.web.tests/Cors/CorsMiddlewareTests.cs.

comments.author: AmeDe
comments.text:
## Cycle 1: Should_ReturnCorsHeaders + Should_HandlePreflightRequest -- Red

Pre-existing failure on main. No code change needed to reach Red -- the tests already fail.

Root cause: TestWebApplicationFactory does not override Cors:AllowedOrigins. The production appsettings.json sets allowed origins to ["https://towncrierapp.uk"], but the tests send Origin: http://localhost:5173. ASP.NET Core CORS middleware correctly refuses to add Access-Control-Allow-Origin for a non-matching origin.

Failing test output (abbreviated):

Should_ReturnCorsHeaders_When_RequestFromAllowedOrigin (1s 228ms):
  Expected response.Headers.Contains("Access-Control-Allow-Origin") to be equal to True
  but found False

Should_HandlePreflightRequest_When_OptionsFromAllowedOrigin:
  Expected response.Headers.Contains("Access-Control-Allow-Origin") to be equal to True
  but found False

Fix plan: Add builder.UseSetting("Cors:AllowedOrigins:0", "http://localhost:5173") in TestWebApplicationFactory.ConfigureWebHost so the test server allows the localhost origin used in tests.

comments.author: AmeDe
comments.text:
## Cycle 1: Should_ReturnCorsHeaders + Should_HandlePreflightRequest -- Green

Fix: Added builder.UseSetting("Cors:AllowedOrigins:0", "http://localhost:5173") to TestWebApplicationFactory.ConfigureWebHost. This overrides the production appsettings.json value so the test server's CORS policy allows the localhost origin used by the tests.

### Cycles Completed
1. Should_ReturnCorsHeaders_When_RequestFromAllowedOrigin + Should_HandlePreflightRequest_When_OptionsFromAllowedOrigin -- Pre-existing Red on main. Fixed by adding Cors:AllowedOrigins:0 config override in TestWebApplicationFactory so the test server allows http://localhost:5173 (the origin used by tests). One-line change in the test factory.

### Root Cause
TestWebApplicationFactory did not override the Cors:AllowedOrigins config section. The production appsettings.json only allows https://towncrierapp.uk. The tests send Origin: http://localhost:5173. ASP.NET Core CORS middleware correctly refused to add the Access-Control-Allow-Origin header because the origin was not in the allowed list. title: Fix PR Gate landmines: missing staticwebapp.config.json and deprecated swift-testing dependency
description:
Two issues on main break the PR Gate for any PR touching web/ or mobile/ios/:

## Root Cause 1: Missing staticwebapp.config.json (Web)
- `web/src/__tests__/static-web-app-config.test.ts` exists on main and reads `web/staticwebapp.config.json` via `readFileSync`
- The config file was never created — the test was committed without its fixture
- Any PR touching `web/` triggers web tests, which crash with ENOENT
- **Fix**: Either create the missing `staticwebapp.config.json` with the expected structure (navigationFallback, globalHeaders), or delete the test if the config is no longer planned

## Root Cause 2: Deprecated swift-testing package (iOS)
- `mobile/ios/Package.swift` uses `swift-tools-version: 6.1` but still declares `swift-testing` 0.12.0 as an explicit dependency
- Swift 6.1 on macOS-15 CI runners includes Swift Testing natively, making the external package redundant
- This causes deprecation warnings on every `@Test` and `@Suite` macro, and caused a `fatalError` crash in run 23672170251
- **Fix**: Remove the `swift-testing` dependency from Package.swift and Package.resolved, and remove the `.product(name: "Testing", package: "swift-testing")` from the test target dependencies

## Evidence
- Affected runs: 23683179285, 23672170251, 23670242752, 23669511830, 23667222895, 23666137229 (and more)
- 4+ failures from missing staticwebapp.config.json, 2+ from iOS swift-testing issues
- Main branch CD Dev succeeds because it does not run tests — only PR Gate catches these
close_reason: Split into two single-domain beads: tc-th4 (web) and tc-8jy (iOS) so autopilot can dispatch each to the correct worker

title: Fix iOS simulator crash: bundleProxyForCurrentProcess is nil for SPM executable target
description: The iOS app crashes on launch in the simulator with: `NSInternalInconsistencyException: bundleProxyForCurrentProcess is nil: mainBundle.bundleURL file:///...Build/Products/Debug-iphonesimulator/`. This happens because the app target is defined as an SPM `.executableTarget` in Package.swift, which does not produce a proper .app bundle with Info.plist, bundle identifier, or entitlements. The iOS simulator requires a real app bundle to launch. The fix likely requires creating an Xcode project (.xcodeproj) for the app target while keeping the library packages (TownCrierDomain, TownCrierData, TownCrierPresentation) as SPM packages. The Xcode project would reference the SPM packages as local dependencies. Environment: CoreSimulator 1051.17.8, iPhone 17 Pro Max simulator, iOS 26.2 (23C54), Xcode on macOS. Note: Auth0 integration was also updated in this session — Auth0Config is now injected via init, and SimpleKeychain is initialised with an explicit service name to avoid a separate Bundle.main.bundleIdentifier force-unwrap crash.

title: Simplify: replace AppCoordinator optional deps with required injection
description:
## What to simplify

/mobile/ios/packages/town-crier-presentation/Sources/Coordinators/AppCoordinator.swift has 8 optional dependencies (lines 17-23) and 3 preconditionFailure calls (lines 90, 109, 124) in factory methods. The pattern is: dependencies are optional at init but required at call-time, crashing at runtime if missing.

Factory methods affected:
- makeOnboardingViewModel() — guards geocoder, watchZoneRepository, onboardingRepository, notificationService
- makeSettingsViewModel() — guards subscriptionService, notificationService  
- makeForceUpdateViewModel() — guards appVersionProvider, versionConfigService

## Why this is simpler

- Compile-time safety instead of runtime crashes via preconditionFailure
- Callers know exactly what's required by constructor signature
- Removes 8 optional unwraps and 3 crash paths
- Tests currently always provide all deps (lines 18-21), masking real-world risk

## Test coverage (CRITICAL)

**Current test status:** AppCoordinatorTests.swift has comprehensive tests but always provides all optional dependencies, so preconditionFailure paths are never hit.

**Before ANY simplification, add tests:**
- [ ] Test that makeOnboardingViewModel works with all required deps provided
- [ ] Test that makeSettingsViewModel works with all required deps provided  
- [ ] Test that makeForceUpdateViewModel works with all required deps provided
- [ ] After making deps required, verify tests still compile and pass

## Suggested approach

Option A (minimal): Make all 8 optional deps required in init. Callers must provide them.
Option B (modular): Create feature-specific coordinators (OnboardingCoordinator, SettingsCoordinator, ForceUpdateCoordinator) with required deps in their init. AppCoordinator delegates.

Option A is simpler and lower-risk. Recommend starting there.

- **azure/login@v2**: 9 inline blocks (cd-dev: 5, cd-prod: 3, pr-gate: 1) replaced with 9 calls to `.github/actions/azure-login`
- **setup-dotnet + NuGet cache**: 4 inline blocks (cd-dev: 2, cd-prod: 1, pr-gate: 1) replaced with 4 calls to `.github/actions/setup-dotnet-infra`
- **Change detection logic**: ~40 lines duplicated in cd-dev and pr-gate replaced with calls to `.github/actions/detect-changes`

### Triggers
- `cd-dev.yml`: push to main (unchanged)
- `cd-prod.yml`: push of v* tags (unchanged)
- `pr-gate.yml`: pull_request to main (unchanged)
- Composite actions have no triggers -- they are called by workflows

### Jobs
All jobs preserved with identical names, conditions, and dependencies. No behavioral changes.

### Required Secrets/Vars
No new secrets or vars required. All existing references preserved as-is.

### Path Filters
detect-changes composite action supports three trigger categories:
- `trigger-all-paths`: triggers all components (api, ios, web, infra)
- `trigger-server-paths`: triggers server components only (api, web, infra)
- `trigger-infra-paths`: triggers only infra
This preserves the original nuanced behavior where pr-gate.yml triggers all 4, cd-dev.yml triggers 3 (no ios), and cd-prod.yml triggers only infra.

comments.author: AmeDe
comments.text:
## Pipeline Evidence

### Workflow Files
- `.github/workflows/web-ci.yml`: Web CI/CD — build, deploy production, PR preview, and preview cleanup

### Validation
- YAML syntax: OK (python3 yaml.safe_load)

### Triggers
- `push` to `main` with `web/**` path filter: runs build + deploy to production
- `pull_request` to `main` with `web/**` path filter: runs build + PR preview staging environment

### Jobs
1. **build**: checkout, setup-node 22, npm ci, tsc --noEmit, npm run build, upload artifact
2. **deploy**: downloads artifact, deploys to Azure SWA with skip_app_build:true (production environment)
3. **preview**: downloads artifact, deploys PR staging environment to Azure SWA
4. **close-preview**: closes the staging environment on PR close

### Concurrency
- Group: web-ci-${{ github.ref }}, cancel-in-progress: true

### Permissions
- Top-level: contents: read (minimal)
- Preview jobs: pull-requests: write

### Required Secrets
- AZURE_STATIC_WEB_APPS_API_TOKEN: Deployment token from Azure Static Web App

### Path Filters
- web/**, .github/workflows/web-ci.yml

title: Simplify: extract DemoAccount seed data from handler
description:
## What to simplify

/api/src/town-crier.application/DemoAccount/GetDemoAccountQueryHandler.cs has an 87-line SeedDemoApplicationsAsync method (lines 67-154) that manually constructs 4 PlanningApplication objects with 11+ hardcoded fields each. Magic numbers include coordinates (-0.1276, 51.5034, etc.), addresses, authority IDs, and dates.

## Why this is simpler

- 4 nearly-identical object construction blocks → a data-driven array or builder
- Magic numbers extracted to named constants or a seed config
- Handler becomes a thin orchestrator (check-if-exists → seed → return), not a data definition file
- Easier to add/modify demo data without touching handler logic

## Test coverage (CRITICAL)

**Current test status:** GetDemoAccountQueryHandler has a test file. Tests verify the handler seeds data and returns results.

Existing handler tests cover the seeding behaviour end-to-end. After extraction:
- [ ] Verify handler tests still pass with extracted seed data
- [ ] Add test that all demo applications have valid coordinates and non-empty addresses

## Suggested approach

1. Create DemoSeedData.cs with a static array of PlanningApplication definitions
2. Move magic numbers to named constants
3. Simplify handler to iterate over the array
4. Run existing tests

title: Simplify: centralise Cosmos DB container name constants (API)
description:
## What to simplify

9 repository files each hardcode GetContainer("town-crier", "ContainerName") with the database name and container name as string literals. Container name casing is inconsistent: 'Users' vs 'groups' vs 'notifications' vs 'DeviceRegistrations' — some PascalCase, some lowercase.

Files: CosmosUserProfileRepository, CosmosGroupRepository, CosmosNotificationRepository, CosmosDeviceRegistrationRepository, CosmosDecisionAlertRepository, CosmosSavedApplicationRepository, CosmosWatchZoneRepository, CosmosPlanningApplicationRepository, CosmosGroupInvitationRepository.

## Why this is simpler

- Single source of truth for database + container names
- Catches casing inconsistencies at compile time
- Renaming a container requires one change

## Test coverage (CRITICAL)

**Current test status:** Repository tests use fakes, not the actual container name resolution. No tests verify container name strings.

This is a structural refactor — extract constants, update references. Existing tests verify repository behaviour unchanged.

## Suggested approach

1. Create CosmosContainerNames static class in town-crier.infrastructure/Cosmos/
2. Define const strings for DatabaseName and each container
3. Replace all 9 hardcoded GetContainer calls
4. Run existing tests

title: Simplify: standardise Wired/Connected page wrapper naming (web)
description:
## What to simplify

16 DI wrapper files use two inconsistent naming conventions for the same pattern:
- 9 use 'Wired' prefix (WiredSettingsPage, WiredNotificationsPage, etc.)
- 4 use 'Connected' prefix (ConnectedDashboardPage, ConnectedSearchPage, etc.)
- 1 uses 'Connected' for onboarding

All do the same thing: inject dependencies via useMemo + context hooks into presentation components.

## Why this is simpler

- One naming convention instead of two for the same pattern
- Reduces confusion for new contributors
- Makes file discovery predictable

## Test coverage (CRITICAL)

**Current test status:** Most wrapper files have tests. This is a rename-only refactor.

Verify all imports and route references are updated. Run full test suite after renaming.

## Suggested approach

1. Pick one convention (suggest 'Connected' — React community standard)
2. Rename all 'Wired' files to 'Connected' (9 files)
3. Update all imports in AppRoutes.tsx and test files
4. Run full test suite

title: Simplify: extract shared iOS formatters (DateFormatter + radiusLabel)
description:
## What to simplify

DateFormatter with 'd MMM yyyy' / en_GB / UTC is duplicated in 3 files:
- ApplicationListRow.swift (lines 8-14)
- ApplicationDetailViewModel.swift (lines 39-45)
- APIPlanningApplicationRepository.swift

radiusLabel() function (metres-to-km/m formatting) is duplicated in 3 files:
- WatchZoneListView.swift (lines 103-111)
- WatchZoneEditorView.swift (lines 119-127)
- RadiusPickerStepView.swift (lines 73-78)
Note: WatchZoneListView uses 'km radius' suffix vs 'km' in the others — inconsistency.

## Why this is simpler

- Eliminates 6 independent copies of 2 formatters
- Single source of truth for date format and radius display
- Fixes inconsistent radius label suffixes

## Test coverage (CRITICAL)

**Current test status:** ApplicationDetailViewModelTests test date formatting indirectly. No direct unit tests for radiusLabel.

**Before ANY simplification, add tests:**
- [ ] Test DateFormatter produces 'd MMM yyyy' for known dates
- [ ] Test radiusLabel for values < 1000 (metres), == 1000, > 1000 (km), fractional km
- [ ] Verify existing ViewModel tests pass after extraction

## Suggested approach

1. Create Date+TownCrier.swift extension with shared dateFormatter
2. Create formatRadius() utility function with consistent suffix
3. Replace all 6 usages, run tests

value: Release skill should run hands-free with no confirmation prompts. The user trusts the commit-based version bump logic and release notes summarization. Never pause for approval during /release.

PERMISSION-FRIENDLY BASH PATTERNS: Pipes and command chaining (&&, ;) break allowlist prefix matching and cause permission prompts. Rules: (1) Never chain different commands with && or ; or | — use separate parallel Bash tool calls instead. (2) Heredoc/EOF syntax is fine and does NOT trigger permission prompts — continue using it for multiline git commit messages and bd descriptions.
